mission.title: Install and Configure Wasmcloud on Kubernetes
mission.description: wasmCloud is an open source Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) project that enables teams to build, manage, and scale polyglot Wasm apps across any cloud, K8s, or edge. Installing wasmCloud allows you to leverage its capabilities for creating scalable and portable applications without vendor lock-in.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: First, create a namespace for wasmCloud to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy the Control Plane
steps.description: Apply the wasmCloud control plane manifests to your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Deploy the Host
steps.description: Next, deploy the wasmCloud host to manage your Wasm applications.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check that the wasmCloud pods are running correctly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of wasmCloud will show the pods running in the 'wasmcloud' namespace when you execute `kubectl get pods --namespace wasmcloud`. You will also be able to access the service via port 8080 on your local machine.

uninstall.title: Remove wasmCloud Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall wasmCloud, delete the resources you created in the wasmcloud namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade wasmCloud
upgrade.description: To upgrade wasmCloud, apply the latest manifests from the repository. Ensure you check for version compatibility.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: If a pod is not starting, check the logs for the pod using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n wasmcloud`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter resource issues, ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated. You can check resource usage with: `kubectl top nodes`.

troubleshooting.title: Networking Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you have networking issues, verify that your cluster's network policies allow communication between pods. Use: `kubectl get networkpolicies -n wasmcloud`.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed to manage it.
